office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Three candidates offer distinct approaches: working-family public investment, expansive universal benefits, and incumbent-led tax and spending restraint.

candidates.summary: Taylor is a small-business owner and caregiver advocating school funding, rural health access, child care, housing affordability, and less corporate influence.
highlights: Calls for fully funded schools and affordable child care [S1]
Criticizes corporate and insurance influence in Olympia [S1]

spending.basis: She supports expanded funding for schools, rural health access, and child care.

experience.basis: She has business and volunteer experience but no documented elected record.

reform.basis: She explicitly criticizes corporate and insurance influence in state government.

candidates.summary: Miller is a planning commissioner and retired power lineman proposing broad price controls, universal benefits, housing rights, and progressive social policies.
highlights: Proposes free health and car insurance, a living wage, and housing as a right [S1]
Supports women's rights, DEI, and fully funded schools [S1]

taxes.basis: He supports eliminating senior property tax and sales tax on necessities alongside expansive public benefits.

spending.basis: He proposes multiple expansive subsidies and universal benefits.

housing.basis: He explicitly describes housing as a right.

experience.basis: He has planning-commission experience but no legislative record.

healthcare.basis: He supports free health insurance, though the dossier does not specify a single-payer mechanism.

social.basis: He explicitly supports women's rights and DEI.

candidates.summary: Marshall is the incumbent representative and former school director, emphasizing tax and spending restraint, public safety, parental authority, and reduced regulation.
highlights: Opposes new taxes and a state income tax [S1]
Supports parental authority, elected sheriffs, and reduced regulation [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly opposes new taxes and a state income tax.

spending.basis: He explicitly supports a restrained state budget.

safety.basis: He prioritizes public safety and elected sheriffs.

experience.basis: He is the incumbent representative and a former elected school director.

local-control.basis: His support for parental authority and elected sheriffs favors local control.

social.basis: He explicitly supports parental authority in schools.
